danger_signals.meaning: Imminent defeat or victory condition approaching
Match progression toward final round; later rounds indicate higher stakes
Primary antagonist and existential threat to Earthrealm
Active combat danger and accumulated damage state

safety_signals.meaning: Temporary reprieve from immediate attack threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Selection screens (character select, difficulty choose, Kombat Kodes)
safety_signals.meaning: Out-of-combat state where player has control and planning time

safety_signals.visual_form: Health bar restoration animation for defeated opponent
safety_signals.meaning: Player-controlled non-lethal decision point; temporary safety moment

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Characters like Liu Kang, Sonya Blade, Jax represent human warriors defending Earthrealm
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban stages (Subway, Bell Tower, The Pit) represent Earthrealm locations under invasion

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Shao Kahn as primary antagonist; Sindel (his resurrected bride) as invasion force component
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Otherworldly or corrupted stage aesthetics indicate Outworld territory or invasion zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Presence of boss character Shao Kahn in arcade ladder; multiple stages representing battle zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI opponents represent warriors responding to invasion threat; storyline progression driven by defeating waves of opponents before facing Shao Kahn

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sindel character model appears as playable/boss character; narrative context in character selection and arcade mode
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Her presence as combatant indicates Shao Kahn's power and orchestration of invasion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Interactive level transitions (uppercuts breaking through ceilings between Subway and Bell Tower, or to Pit 3)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stage transitions represent dynamic combat geography; continuing fight in new environment signals increased intensity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: No tournament bracket UI or ceremonial stage design; replaced by invasion/battle sequence logic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gameplay flow is continuous martial response rather than tournament progression

environmental_storytelling.example: Urban stages (Subway, Bell Tower) and natural stages (The Pit) communicate that invasion affects both civilization and wilderness
Uppercut-triggered ceiling breaks and stage transitions show environmental instability during invasion; game world is literally breaking apart
Earthrealm defenders (Liu Kang, Sonya, Jax) vs. Outworld invaders visually represent the conflict through available playable characters
Single-player arcade mode culminates in Shao Kahn confrontation, visually and narratively communicating him as the core threat

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Appears at end of arcade ladder; represents culminating threat that all other matches build toward
Can utilize Run button (with meter) to close distance; demonstrates agency in combat pacing
Can receive Mercy (health restoration), unlocking option to perform Animality (transformation finishing move)
Depletes when running or performing combos; visual indicator of movement/offense sustainability

notes: Mortal Kombat 3 (SNES) communicates world-state primarily through narrative framing (Shao Kahn invasion replacing tournament structure) and stage selection rather than persistent environmental visual language. The game does not employ the sophisticated visual semiotics for faction identity or political situation that would be expected in later action-RPGs or narrative-heavy games. Instead, it relies on character roster selection and boss structure to convey conflict. Interactive stage transitions (ceiling breaks via uppercuts) provide the most sophisticated environmental storytelling mechanic, physically demonstrating that the world is destabilized by invasion. Kombat Kodes system operates as meta-communication for unlocking hidden features, not as world-state manifestation. No color-coding or sigil systems evident in available sources. The paradigm is pure fighting-game competition; world-state is secondary to mechanical gameplay.
sources_used: wikipedia - Mortal Kombat 3 gameplay mechanics, plot, characters, and features
